raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"The artifact contains an apparent contradiction between the reported decrease in self-preference and the stated positive causal interpretation, so logical consistency is central to cleaning it up.","R1.3":"The delegation implicitly requires checking whether the stated interpretation matches the reported accuracy and preference results.","R1.6":"The phrase \"Clean this up\" most directly requests sentence- and paragraph-level improvement of clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Any projected cleanup responsibility must preserve uncertainty around causal interpretation and statistical support.","RX.2":"The artifact makes strong causal and monotone claims, so avoiding overclaim is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The responsibility should remain limited to cleanup of the given research passage rather than adding new analysis or external validation.","RX.4":"Misstating causal evidence or mitigation implications could mislead downstream scientific or evaluation practice.","RX.5":"The cleanup must remain traceable to the reported numbers, controls, and claims in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an apparent contradiction between the reported decrease in self-preference and the stated positive causal interpretation, so logical consistency is central to cleaning it up.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation implicitly requires checking whether the stated interpretation matches the reported accuracy and preference results.
rationale.R1.6: The phrase "Clean this up" most directly requests sentence- and paragraph-level improvement of clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected cleanup responsibility must preserve uncertainty around causal interpretation and statistical support.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact makes strong causal and monotone claims, so avoiding overclaim is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility should remain limited to cleanup of the given research passage rather than adding new analysis or external validation.
rationale.RX.4: Misstating causal evidence or mitigation implications could mislead downstream scientific or evaluation practice.
rationale.RX.5: The cleanup must remain traceable to the reported numbers, controls, and claims in the provided artifact.
